builds.mindscapes:
M1 gives 10% RES PEN when using One's Path or Earth Shaker, 10% Sheer DMG% when using certain skills; using Crushing Peaks extends stun duration by 2s on stunned target, which can be good for short stun duration target but can lose out another stun when used with Dialyn, making it situational.

Low spenders should only get his Signature W-Engine.
builds.other_notes:
High-level gameplay for Banyue requires executing his Perfect Cancel well (adding Basic/EX input at the correct time while another animation is running) to fit in the most DMG. Each version of EX skill has different animation length and scaling, so what combo to use during a stun duration is dependant on the enemy. Focus on fitting the free EX skills and an enhanced EX skill in stun time.
Currently, it's recommended to loop EX: Earth Shaker when out of stun as it has the highest scaling but of course it depend on the flow of battle. Sub-Optimal gameplay can still clear end-game.

builds.other_notes:
Dialyn's Chain-to-Ult conversion works as follows: She gains 1 Customer Complaint when an Ultimate is used or upon reaching 90 Positive Reviews. She generates 0.6 Positive Reviews per second and 7.5 per EX.
Upon reaching 90: Triggering a Chain Attack window will convert the next Agent’s Chain into their Ultimate, consuming 60 Positive Reviews if done when the enemy is Stunned. If her Positive Reviews are less than 90, she can hold Basic Attack to consume Customer Complaint, triggering a Quick Assist instead. Outside-of-Stun, if Dialyn has 90+ Positive Reviews, she triggers a Chain Attack window instead of a Quick Assist, upgrading the next Chain Attack into an Ultimate.

builds.w_engine_notes:
- Cordis Germina is Harumasa's BiS in Dialyn team, giving 25% Elec DMG and DEF ignore on Ultimate. the downside is that it's hard not to overcap CR%, as Harumasa on Seed's sig has 83.4% CR on his Dash Attack already without a single CR% substat roll. His own signature is still better outside of Dialyn team by a bit. 
- Brimstone offers high combat ATK%, and can sometimes outperform Crit statstick as Harumasa can stacks Brimstone buff really well, and he gains a lot of Crit stats from Core Passive.
builds.mindscapes:
M1 allows more stacks from your basic attack and your quivers shooting another arrow at the target, which makes him able to do a few more Dashes during stun window, and improving his on field/out of stun capability. M2 gives his own dash attacks 50% DMG buff after using Chain Attack/Ultimate. 
it's recommended to Vertically invest in supportive agent's mindscapes first (such as Dialyn, Trigger, Astra, etc.) before pulling Harumasa's mindscapes.
builds.other_notes:
Harumasa's main game plan is to fit an Ultimate and as many Enhanced Dash Attacks (EDA for short) as possible during the stun window. 
Before entering the battle, Harumasa can pre-generate 6 Electro Quivers via doing his Basic Attack strings 3 times. If there's already 6 Quivers on field, Harumasa can use a Chain Attack/EX Special to generate 6 more Quivers, which makes the previous Quivers fired to the enemy and mark it. by doing this you can save times from his aiming attack, allowing more EDAs in stun.

builds.w_engine_notes:
When using Deep Sea Visitor or Severed Innocence, make sure you use Basic Attack once before you EX/Ult so you trigger the W-Engine's CR%/CD% passive.
When using Steel Cushion, you can use his EX to let him go behind the enemy, then quickly cancel it with Ultimate before he depletes the energy. this way his Ultimate can benefit from Steel Cushion's passive.
builds.mindscapes:
M1 In Dark Abyss Reverb state, totalize skill gets an extra 30% CD and 12% CR
M2 When Ult triggers totalize, it doesn't end the stun window, allowing an EX to be used as well, effectively doubling your damage in that stun window. Totalize damage also gains 15% DEF Ignore.
builds.other_notes:
Hugo has a pretty straightforward playstyle: Stun the enemy, then use double Chain Attack with Hugo, get Dark Abyss Reverb state, then EX/Ult which triggers Totalize and ends the stun state, rinse and repeat. 
Note that even without Lighter's stun extension, you still want to use double Chain Attack since it still nets more damage than skipping the 2nd Chain for slightly more Totalize Damage, unless the stun window has a short duration.
builds.team_general: Ideally used with double stunners to get as many Totalized as you can while also getting the extra ATK from having 1/2 stunners in his team

²ER% stat stick is better if it allows for an additional Stun phase. Otherwise, use an alternative option mentioned. If ran alongside an Abloom focused DPS, do not use an ER% Stat Stick or Boisterous Echoes.
Flamemaker Shaker is a valid choice, but can be difficult to optimize and is not worth picking up from Collectible Exchange.
builds.mindscapes:
M1: Gives Velina's Vortex 20% All-Attribute RES Ignore. The whole Squad's Windswept damage gets 20% Wind Attribute RES Ignore. Daze from EX: Wide-Area Cyclone is increased by 20%.
M2: Triggering Windswept will grant Velina 1 Windbite (5s cd). Triggering Coloring will build Anomaly for the teammate's Attribute (does not contribute to damage). Windswept/Vortex DMG bonus from AA increases by 15%.
M0R1 is a good stopping point, but her M1 and M2 are great vertical investment choices.
builds.other_notes:
Applying a Wind Anomaly inflicts the Windswept status, and applying a different Attribute Anomaly on top of it does not trigger a Disorder, but instead triggers Vortex. This means that agents who rely on Disorder, such as Miyabi, have poor synergy with Velina, as gaining resources becomes much harder.
Along with Miyabi, Alice and Yanagi are not great partners with Velina. Alice due to no Disorders for Blade Etiquette generation, and Yanagi due to Wind's Disorder MV being very low.
builds.team_general: Best with Agents that have fast Anomaly Buildup to make full use of the Vortex mechanic. Generic team example: Anomaly not reliant on Disorders alongside a Support (or Zhao)

builds.mindscapes:
M1: Increases the gain of Flight Feather, increases the Attribute Anomaly and Disorder DMG% of the team. (decent)
M2: Increases Vivian's Anomaly Buildup Rate, her Abloom scaling is increased and ignores 15% RES. (amazing)
M6: Further enhances Flight Feather gain rate, increases Ether DMG% and multiplicatively boost Abloom DMG without fear of Feather stacks overcap. (amazing)
M2 is the recommended stopping point for those who choose to invest in Mindscapes, but her M6 is also amazing if you want to really invest into her.
builds.other_notes:
Although Core states "per 10 point of Anomaly Proficiency", each point of AP is taken into account for her Abloom scaling.
Fire, Electric, Ice, and Physical DMG% on Vivian don't affect the respective Attribute Abloom DMG.
When swapped out during Noblewoman state, Vivian automatically converts her Flight Feather into Guard Feather. Use a Quick Assist or her Basic Attack string to quickly convert her feathers at the start of battle.

builds.other_notes:

Attack/Ultimate(Lvl these). Maintain her Shinrabanshou state and make use of Dodge Counters/Assists to let Yanagi skip to the 3rd Basic Attack in her Basic Attack chain increasing her DMG output.

builds.mindscapes:
M1: Enables Ye Shunguang to immediately enter the Enlightened state when entering battle, and further amplifies her damage. (decent)
M2: Allows Ye Shunguang to overcap up to 6 stacks of Qingming Sword Force instead of 3, and increases the amount of EX Special Attack: Soaring Light that can be used while preserving the same amount of Basic Attacks that use Sword Force. EX Special: Soaring Light and Ultimate: Cleaving Heavens ignore 40% of enemy DEF. (amazing)
If you want to invest, M0W1 is the recommended stopping point.
builds.other_notes:
Ye Shunguang plays around her Enlightened state, activated by getting 6 stack of Qingming Sword Force. She gains stacks through Basic Attacks, with additional points granted if you Perfect Dodge using her Special.
Full stacks can be given from the following sources: 1 upon using her EX, 6 upon using Ult outside of Enlightened state, and 3 from her Additional Ability. Excess Qingming Sword Force stacks are converted to Bearer, including those gained while in Enlightened state. Upon ending the state, those are converted back to Qingming Sword Force up to 3 before M2, so make sure to not waste excess stacks.

M1 reduces Yidhari's Ex Special Attack: Glacial Crush's Adrenaline cost by 10, and lets you use 35 more Adrenaline to use a second Glacial Crush, increasing its healing and ignoring 20% Ice Res. (amazing)

M6 grants her a 25% Sheer Damage increase and a guardian angel mechanic. (ok)
builds.other_notes: As with all Rupture agents, Def Shred and Pen are completely useless for Yidhari — keep that in mind for teambuilding. Lighter is a "usable" teammate option, but he requires a Faction member or an Attacker (Orphie) in order to get his AA. Lacking his AA requires compensation, such as using his Sig, in order to attain good scores.

builds.mindscapes:
M1 - Provides 10% in-combat CR% while allowing her to start the fight with full Talisman bar. In addition to follow up attack whenever another agent deal damage to an enemy target with 6 sec Cooldown
M2 -  Ultimate/EX Special now ignore 15% Ether res and casting a decibel Ult on a stunned target, extend their stun duration for 3 sec. After Casting Talisman Ult, gain 1 cast of special EX that deal 1200% Sheer Force
M6 - Casting Ult allows you to bypass the resource cost of Talisman Ult with a cooldown of 30 sec, further more increase Yixuan Sheer Force by 20% during her Meditation(AA) state.
builds.other_notes: Rina is not a teammate for Yixuan as Sheer Damage ignores all DEF. All ATK buffers are good for Yixuan despite her scaling off of HP as Sheer converts 30% of your ATK into Sheer Force. For example Lucy's 600 ATK will convert to 180 Sheer Force. Nicole is suprisingly a strong support for Yixuan, as she gives CR% (M6), Ether DMG% Bonus (AA), and more DMG% from her Wengine.
builds.team_general: Any 2 Supports/Supportive agents or 1 Support + 1 Stunner to help out Yixuan's Damage
